metadata.title: LT: How to Set Up Your ForUsAll 401(k) Account
metadata.description: Comprehensive guidance for helping a participant set up their ForUsAll 401(k) online account once their ForUsAll profile is available for setup. Covers setup prerequisites, email matching, activation link and password creation, mandatory MFA with Google Authenticator or Okta Verify (including the second-device QR-code requirement), future login flow, and dashboard features including savings rate updates, portfolio changes, statements, beneficiary management, loans and distributions (when plan- and eligibility-permitted), and ForUsAll Participant Support contact options.

rules: To create a ForUsAll online account, the participant must have a profile created at ForUsAll which is taken from the employer's payroll platform or payroll file.

To create a ForUsAll online account, the participant needs access to the email address listed on their employer's payroll platform or payroll files, a computer or laptop, their mobile phone, and an authenticator app installed on their phone such as Google Authenticator or Okta Verify.
The participant's email address must match the email their employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll.

A participant can set up their ForUsAll online account once their profile is available for account setup; receiving an initial enrollment notice or seeing an onboarding_email is helpful confirmation but should not be treated as the only way to confirm setup can proceed.
The participant should go to forusall.com/login and select 'New here? Create an Account' at the bottom of the login page if they are a new user.

If the entered email address and any required identity verification information match the system, the participant will receive or continue through an activation flow with instructions to complete registration.
If the email, last 4 of SSN, or last name do not match, or if the participant is unsure which email to use, they should contact their HR manager or ForUsAll Participant Support.

After completing identity verification if prompted and creating a password through the activation or registration flow, the participant should return to forusall.com/login and log in with their email address and new password.
ForUsAll requires multi-factor authentication, also called MFA, during account setup.
MFA requires a one-time authentication code in addition to the participant's email address and password.
The authentication code is generated by an authenticator app on the participant's phone.

The article lists Google Authenticator and Okta Verify as the supported authenticator apps for this article's standard first-time setup flow. The broader ForUsAll MFA article (LT: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for Your ForUsAll 401(k) Account) covers additional supported options such as Microsoft Authenticator, Authy, and the browser-based Authenticator.cc; defer to that article for participants who want or already use another supported app.

For the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow, the participant needs both a computer or laptop and their phone because the QR code displays on one device and is scanned with the authenticator app on the phone. If the participant does not have a second device available, refer them to the browser-based Authenticator.cc option documented in 'LT: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for Your ForUsAll 401(k) Account', or redirect them to ForUsAll Participant Support.
For Google Authenticator, the article identifies the correct app icon as the multicolor star.

The participant should open Google Authenticator, tap the + sign, select Scan a QR code, scan the QR code shown on the ForUsAll login page, and enter the six-digit code displayed by the app on the ForUsAll website.
If the participant cannot scan the QR code, they may select the option to enter a setup key manually.
For Okta Verify, the article identifies the correct app icon as a blue circle with a check mark.
The participant should not use Okta Mobile for this setup.

The participant should open Okta Verify, tap Add or the + icon, select Organization, select Skip when asked Add Account from Another Device?, select Yes, Ready to Scan when asked Do you have a QR Code?, scan the QR code shown on the ForUsAll login page, and enter the six-digit code displayed by the app on the ForUsAll website.
If needed, the participant can choose the option to enter the key manually after selecting Yes, Ready to Scan.
After the account and MFA are set up, the participant should log in at forusall.com/login.
For future logins, the participant will enter their email address, password, and the authentication code from the app used during setup.
Once logged in, the participant will see their ForUsAll dashboard.

The dashboard may include account balance, savings rate, portfolio snapshot, transaction history, links to change rates, My Portfolio, Loans & Distributions, Statements, Beneficiary Information, FAQ, and Settings.
From the dashboard, the participant can select Change My Savings Rates to update pre-tax contribution, Roth contribution, or both.
Savings rates may be entered as a percentage, or the participant may switch to a dollar value if that option is available.
The participant can choose to enroll in auto-escalation and select Update to save the election.
From My Portfolio, the participant can select Explore Funds, review available fund options, update investment percentages, and use fund hyperlinks or ticker symbols to research available investments.

To view recent statements, the participant can select the three dots in the top right corner of the dashboard and then select Statements. Statement posting timing is documented in the statement_access_and_delivery category.
To add or update a beneficiary, the participant can select the three dots in the top right corner of the dashboard and then select Beneficiaries.
The beneficiary feature becomes available once the participant's first contribution has been deposited.
If the participant adds multiple beneficiaries, the total allocation must equal 100%.
If the plan allows loans or withdrawals and the participant is eligible, the participant can select Loans & Distributions from the top right corner of the dashboard.
Participants can manage future statement delivery from the Statements section.

Statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter. For exact per-quarter posting dates and on-demand statement requests, defer to 'LT: How to Access and Manage Your ForUsAll 401(k) Statements and Beneficiaries'.
ForUsAll recommends adding at least one beneficiary as soon as possible once the beneficiary feature is available.
A primary beneficiary is the person or people who receive benefits from the plan in the event of the participant's passing.
A contingent beneficiary is the person or people who may receive benefits if the primary beneficiary is deceased.
For withdrawals, participants may select from the eligible withdrawal or termination distribution reasons shown.
For loans, the maximum amount available to borrow will be displayed.
Participants may access the Quick Loan Calculator and review loan specifications under What you should know before proceeding.
Participants should contact ForUsAll Participant Support if they have trouble creating their account.
Participants should contact ForUsAll Participant Support if they do not receive their activation email.
Participants should contact ForUsAll Participant Support if they are unsure which email address to use.
Participants should contact ForUsAll Participant Support if they need help with MFA setup.

steps.description: Confirm the participant has a ForUsAll profile by checking their first name, last name, company name, and email address. Also check the Communications section in the participant profile to confirm whether an onboarding_email was sent and note the sent date in YYYY-MM-DD format.
steps.notes: The participant can set up the account once their profile is available for account setup. An onboarding_email or initial enrollment notice is helpful confirmation, but it is not the only way to confirm setup can proceed. If the participant cannot find the link or confirms the link is expired, direct the participant to support.
New users should select 'New here? Create an Account' link at the bottom of the login page.

steps.description: Enter the email address associated with the ForUsAll account.
steps.notes: The email must match the email the employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll. If the email does not match or the participant is unsure which email to use, they should contact their HR manager or ForUsAll Participant Support.

steps.description: Follow the registration prompts and verify identity using last name and the last 4 digits of the Social Security number if prompted.
steps.notes: If the email address and required identity information match the system, the participant can continue with the activation or registration flow. If an error appears, direct the participant to ForUsAll Participant Support.

steps.description: Open the activation email if sent and follow the prompts to create a password.
steps.notes: Identity verification should be completed before password creation if prompted during registration. After the password is created, continue to login.

steps.description: Return to forusall.com/login and log in using the email address and new password.
steps.notes: This follows identity verification and password creation through the activation or registration flow.

steps.description: The website will display a QR code to set up multi-factor authentication. Participants may use Google Authenticator or Okta Verify.
steps.notes: For the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow, use both a computer or laptop and a phone because the QR code appears on one device and is scanned with the authenticator app on the phone. If the participant does not have a second device available, direct them to ForUsAll Participant Support.

steps.description: If using Google Authenticator, open the app, tap the + sign, select Scan a QR code, scan the QR code shown on the ForUsAll login page, and enter the six-digit code on the ForUsAll website.
steps.notes: The correct Google Authenticator app icon is the multicolor star. If the QR code cannot be scanned, the participant may use the option to enter a setup key manually.

steps.description: If using Okta Verify, open the app, tap Add or the + icon, select Organization, select Skip when asked Add Account from Another Device?, select Yes, Ready to Scan when asked Do you have a QR Code?, scan the QR code shown on the ForUsAll login page, and enter the six-digit code on the ForUsAll website.
steps.notes: The correct Okta Verify app icon is a blue circle with a check mark. Do not use Okta Mobile. If needed, the participant can enter the key manually after selecting Yes, Ready to Scan.

steps.description: For future logins, go to forusall.com/login and enter the email address, password, and authentication code from the authenticator app used during setup.
steps.notes: After login, the participant will see the ForUsAll dashboard.

steps.description: Use the dashboard to access available features, such as savings rate changes, My Portfolio, statements, beneficiaries, loans, and distributions, when those features are available and applicable.
steps.notes: The dashboard may include different features, and loans or withdrawals depend on whether the plan allows them and whether the participant is eligible.

common_issues.issue: Participant is unsure which email address is associated with their account.
common_issues.resolution: Tell the participant that the email must match the email their employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll, and direct them to contact their HR team or ForUsAll Participant Support.

common_issues.issue: Participant enters an email address that does not match the system.
common_issues.resolution: Direct the participant to contact their HR manager or ForUsAll Participant Support.

common_issues.issue: Participant does not receive the activation email.
common_issues.resolution: Direct the participant to ForUsAll Participant Support, because the article lists not receiving the activation email as a reason to contact support.

common_issues.issue: Participant has trouble with MFA setup.
common_issues.resolution: Guide the participant through the Google Authenticator or Okta Verify steps in the article, share the MFA setup video URL if helpful, and direct them to ForUsAll Participant Support if they still need help.

common_issues.issue: Participant cannot scan the MFA QR code.
common_issues.resolution: Tell the participant they may select the option to enter a setup key manually.

common_issues.issue: Participant is using Okta Mobile instead of Okta Verify.
common_issues.resolution: Tell the participant to use Okta Verify, identified in the article as the app with a blue circle and a check mark, and not to use Okta Mobile.

common_issues.issue: Participant asks why Beneficiaries is not available.
common_issues.resolution: Explain that the beneficiary feature becomes available once the participant's first contribution has been deposited.

common_issues.issue: Participant asks about loans or withdrawals but does not see or cannot use the option.
common_issues.resolution: Explain that Loans & Distributions is available if the plan allows loans or withdrawals and the participant is eligible; do not guarantee availability without confirming plan allowance and eligibility.

common_issues.issue: Participant has trouble creating their password after opening the activation email.
common_issues.resolution: Tell the participant to follow the prompts in the activation email to create their password. If they are unable to complete the password setup, direct them to ForUsAll Participant Support.

common_issues.issue: Participant is unsure when they can create their account.
common_issues.resolution: Explain that account setup can proceed once their profile is available for account setup. If they have not received an initial enrollment notice, confirm internal setup indicators such as onboarding_email or redirect to ForUsAll Participant Support if setup status is unclear.

common_issues.issue: Participant does not understand why MFA is required.
common_issues.resolution: Explain that ForUsAll requires multi-factor authentication during account setup for added security. MFA protects the account by requiring a one-time authentication code they only have access to in addition to the participant's email address and password.

common_issues.issue: Participant is unsure which Google Authenticator app to use.
common_issues.resolution: Tell the participant to use the Google Authenticator app with the multicolor star icon.

common_issues.issue: Participant is setting up Okta Verify and is asked whether they are adding an account from another device.
common_issues.resolution: Tell the participant to select Skip when asked Add Account from Another Device?, then select Yes, Ready to Scan when asked whether they have a QR code.

common_issues.issue: Participant needs help logging in after account setup and MFA setup are complete.
common_issues.resolution: Tell the participant to go to forusall.com/login and enter their email address, password, and the authentication code from the app they used during MFA setup.

common_issues.issue: Participant does not see every dashboard feature listed in the article.
common_issues.resolution: Explain that the dashboard may include features such as account balance, savings rate, portfolio snapshot, transaction history, links to change rates, My Portfolio, Loans & Distributions, Statements, Beneficiary Information, FAQ, and Settings. Do not guarantee that every listed feature will appear for every participant.

common_issues.issue: Participant wants to update their savings rate.
common_issues.resolution: Direct the participant to select Change My Savings Rates from the dashboard. Explain that they can update their pre-tax contribution, Roth contribution, or both, and then select Update to save the election.

common_issues.issue: Participant wants to enter a dollar value instead of a percentage for their savings rate.
common_issues.resolution: Explain that the participant may enter their savings rate as a percentage, or switch to a dollar value if that option is available. Do not guarantee that the dollar value option is available for every participant.

common_issues.issue: Participant wants to change or review their portfolio.
common_issues.resolution: Direct the participant to select My Portfolio, then Explore Funds. Explain that they can review available fund options, update investment percentages, and use fund hyperlinks or ticker symbols to research available investments.

common_issues.issue: Participant wants to view statements.
common_issues.resolution: Tell the participant to select the three dots in the top right corner of the dashboard and then select Statements. Explain that statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter.

common_issues.issue: Participant asks why a recent statement is not available yet.
common_issues.resolution: Explain that statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter. Do not provide a different timeline unless it is stated in the article.

common_issues.issue: Participant wants to add multiple beneficiaries.
common_issues.resolution: Explain that multiple beneficiaries can be added when the feature is available, but the total allocation must equal 100%.

common_issues.issue: Participant asks the difference between primary and contingent beneficiaries.
common_issues.resolution: Explain that a primary beneficiary is the person or people who receive benefits from the plan in the event of the participant's passing. A contingent beneficiary is the person or people who may receive benefits if the primary beneficiary is deceased.

common_issues.issue: Participant wants to know how loan amounts are shown.
common_issues.resolution: Explain that, for loans, the maximum amount available to borrow will be displayed if the participant can access the loan flow.

common_issues.issue: Participant wants to know what withdrawal reasons are available.
common_issues.resolution: Explain that, for withdrawals, the participant may select from the eligible withdrawal or termination distribution reasons shown in the Loans & Distributions section if the plan allows withdrawals and the participant is eligible.

examples.scenario: A participant has received the initial enrollment notice but is not yet eligible to contribute.
examples.outcome: The participant can set up the ForUsAll online account because their profile is available for account setup; contribution eligibility is not required.

examples.scenario: A participant enters an email address and the system does not recognize it.
examples.outcome: The participant should contact their HR manager or ForUsAll Participant Support because the email must match the employer or payroll provider email on file with ForUsAll.

examples.scenario: A participant wants to use Okta for MFA and asks which Okta app to download.
examples.outcome: The participant should use Okta Verify, identified by a blue circle with a check mark, and should not use Okta Mobile.

examples.scenario: A participant cannot scan the QR code during MFA setup.
examples.outcome: The participant may select the option to enter a setup key manually.

examples.scenario: A participant wants to add multiple beneficiaries.
examples.outcome: The participant can add multiple beneficiaries when the beneficiary feature is available, but the total allocation must equal 100%.

examples.scenario: A participant asks whether they can take a loan from the dashboard.
examples.outcome: The agent may say the participant can select Loans & Distributions if the plan allows loans or withdrawals and the participant is eligible, but must not guarantee loan availability.

examples.scenario: A participant has not received their initial enrollment notice and asks whether they can create an account.
examples.outcome: The agent should not treat the missing initial enrollment notice alone as a definitive blocker. Confirm internally whether the participant profile and onboarding_email indicate setup can proceed, and redirect to ForUsAll Participant Support if setup status is unclear.

examples.scenario: A participant is unsure which email address to enter during account creation.
examples.outcome: The participant should use the email address their employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll. If they are unsure, they should contact their HR team or ForUsAll Participant Support.

examples.scenario: A participant receives the activation email and needs to create a password.
examples.outcome: The participant should open the activation email, follow the prompts, complete identity verification if prompted, create a password, then return to forusall.com/login and log in with their email address and new password.

examples.scenario: A participant asks why MFA is required during setup.
examples.outcome: The agent may explain that ForUsAll requires MFA for added security because it protects the account by requiring a one-time authentication code in addition to the participant's email address and password.

examples.scenario: A participant wants to use Google Authenticator for MFA setup.
examples.outcome: The participant should use the Google Authenticator app with the multicolor star icon, tap the + sign, select Scan a QR code, scan the QR code shown on the ForUsAll login page, and enter the six-digit code on the ForUsAll website.

examples.scenario: A participant is setting up Okta Verify and is asked whether they are adding an account from another device.
examples.outcome: The participant should select Skip, then select Yes, Ready to Scan when asked whether they have a QR code.

examples.scenario: A participant asks how to log in after account setup and MFA are complete.
examples.outcome: The participant should go to forusall.com/login and enter their email address, password, and the authentication code from the app used during MFA setup.

examples.scenario: A participant wants to update their savings rate.
examples.outcome: The participant should select Change My Savings Rates from the dashboard, update their pre-tax contribution, Roth contribution, or both, and select Update to save the election.

examples.scenario: A participant wants to enter a dollar amount instead of a percentage for their savings rate.
examples.outcome: The participant may enter the savings rate as a percentage or switch to a dollar value if that option is available. The agent should not guarantee the dollar value option is available.

examples.scenario: A participant wants to review or change their investment portfolio.
examples.outcome: The participant should select My Portfolio, then Explore Funds. They can review available fund options, update investment percentages, and use fund hyperlinks or ticker symbols to research available investments.

examples.scenario: A participant wants to view statements.
examples.outcome: The participant should select the three dots in the top right corner of the dashboard and then select Statements. The agent may explain that statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter.

examples.scenario: A participant asks why a recent statement is not available yet.
examples.outcome: The agent may explain that statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter and should not provide a different timeline.

examples.scenario: A participant asks why the Beneficiaries section is not available.
examples.outcome: The agent may explain that the beneficiary feature becomes available once the participant's first contribution has been deposited.

examples.scenario: A participant asks the difference between a primary and contingent beneficiary.
examples.outcome: The agent may explain that a primary beneficiary receives benefits from the plan in the event of the participant's passing, while a contingent beneficiary may receive benefits if the primary beneficiary is deceased.

examples.scenario: A participant asks what happens when accessing loans from the dashboard.
examples.outcome: If the plan allows loans and the participant is eligible, the maximum amount available to borrow will be displayed, and the participant may access the Quick Loan Calculator and review loan specifications under What you should know before proceeding.

examples.scenario: A participant asks what withdrawal reasons are available.
examples.outcome: If the plan allows withdrawals and the participant is eligible, the participant may select from the eligible withdrawal or termination distribution reasons shown in Loans & Distributions.

notes: Statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter.
The participant needs access to the email address connected to their ForUsAll 401(k) account.
The participant needs a computer or laptop, a mobile phone, and an authenticator app such as Google Authenticator or Okta Verify.
The MFA setup video is available at https://www.forusall.com/sign-up/2fa.
Google Authenticator and Okta Verify are the authenticator apps listed in the article.
Authentication codes are time-based one-time passcodes (TOTP) that refresh about every 30 seconds, so the participant should use the newest code shown in the app and enter it before it refreshes.
ForUsAll recommends adding at least one beneficiary as soon as possible.
The beneficiary feature becomes available once the first contribution has been deposited.
If multiple beneficiaries are added, the total allocation must equal 100%.
The dashboard may include different features.
Loans and withdrawals depend on whether the plan allows them and whether the participant is eligible.

Participants can set up their ForUsAll online account once their profile is available for account setup. Receiving an initial enrollment notice or seeing an onboarding_email is helpful confirmation, but it is not the only way to confirm setup can proceed.
The email address entered during setup must match the email the participant's employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll.
If the participant is unsure which email is associated with their account, they should contact their HR team or ForUsAll Participant Support.
For future logins, participants must enter their email address, password, and the authentication code from the app used during MFA setup.
Participants can access savings rate changes, My Portfolio, statements, beneficiaries, loans, and distributions from the dashboard when those features are available and applicable.

faq_pairs.question: When can a participant create their ForUsAll online account?
faq_pairs.answer: A participant can set up their ForUsAll online account once their profile is available for account setup. Receiving an initial enrollment notice or seeing an onboarding_email is helpful confirmation, but it should not be treated as the only way to confirm setup can proceed.

faq_pairs.question: What does a participant need before creating their online account?
faq_pairs.answer: They need access to the email address connected to their ForUsAll 401(k) account, a computer or laptop, a mobile phone, and an authenticator app such as Google Authenticator or Okta Verify.

faq_pairs.question: What should a participant do if they are unsure which email address to use?
faq_pairs.answer: They should contact their HR team or ForUsAll Participant Support. The email must match the email their employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll.

faq_pairs.question: What should a participant do if their email does not match the system?
faq_pairs.answer: They should contact their HR manager or ForUsAll Participant Support.

faq_pairs.question: What happens after a participant enters the correct email address?
faq_pairs.answer: If the email address and any required identity verification information match the system, the participant will receive or continue through an activation flow with instructions to complete registration.

faq_pairs.question: What should a participant do after receiving the activation email?
faq_pairs.answer: They should open the activation email, follow the prompts, complete identity verification if prompted, create their password, then return to forusall.com/login and log in using their email address and new password.

faq_pairs.question: Why is MFA required during account setup?
faq_pairs.answer: ForUsAll requires multi-factor authentication for added security. MFA helps protect the account by requiring a one-time authentication code in addition to the participant's email address and password.

faq_pairs.question: Which authenticator apps can be used for MFA setup?
faq_pairs.answer: The article lists Google Authenticator and Okta Verify.

faq_pairs.question: What is the recommended way to complete MFA setup?
faq_pairs.answer: For the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow, the participant needs both a computer or laptop and their phone. The QR code displays on the computer, and the participant scans it using the authenticator app on their phone. If the participant does not have a second device available, redirect them to ForUsAll Participant Support.

faq_pairs.question: How does a participant set up MFA with Google Authenticator?
faq_pairs.answer: The participant should use the Google Authenticator app with the multicolor star icon, open the app, tap the + sign, select Scan a QR code, scan the QR code shown on the ForUsAll login page, and enter the six-digit code on the ForUsAll website.

faq_pairs.question: Can a participant use Okta Mobile for MFA setup?
faq_pairs.answer: No. The article says to use Okta Verify and not to use Okta Mobile.

faq_pairs.question: How does a participant set up MFA with Okta Verify?
faq_pairs.answer: The participant should use the Okta Verify app with the blue circle and check mark icon, tap Add or the + icon, select Organization, select Skip when asked Add Account from Another Device?, select Yes, Ready to Scan when asked Do you have a QR Code?, scan the QR code shown on the ForUsAll login page, and enter the six-digit code on the ForUsAll website.

faq_pairs.question: What should a participant do if they cannot scan the MFA QR code?
faq_pairs.answer: They may select the option to enter a setup key manually.

faq_pairs.question: Is there an MFA setup video?
faq_pairs.answer: Yes. The MFA setup video is available at https://www.forusall.com/sign-up/2fa.

faq_pairs.question: How does a participant log in after account and MFA setup are complete?
faq_pairs.answer: For future logins, the participant should go to forusall.com/login and enter their email address, password, and the authentication code from the app used during setup.

faq_pairs.question: What may a participant see on their ForUsAll dashboard?
faq_pairs.answer: The dashboard may include account balance, savings rate, portfolio snapshot, transaction history, links to change rates, My Portfolio, Loans & Distributions, Statements, Beneficiary Information, FAQ, and Settings.

faq_pairs.question: How can a participant update their savings rate?
faq_pairs.answer: From the dashboard, the participant should select Change My Savings Rates. They can update their pre-tax contribution, Roth contribution, or both, and select Update to save the election.

faq_pairs.question: Can a participant enter a dollar value instead of a percentage for their savings rate?
faq_pairs.answer: The participant may enter their savings rate as a percentage, or switch to a dollar value if that option is available. The article does not guarantee that the dollar value option is available for every participant.

faq_pairs.question: Can a participant enroll in auto-escalation from the dashboard?
faq_pairs.answer: Yes. The article says the participant can choose to enroll in auto-escalation when updating their savings rate, then select Update to save the election.

faq_pairs.question: How can a participant view or change their portfolio?
faq_pairs.answer: From the dashboard, the participant should select My Portfolio, then select Explore Funds. They can review available fund options, update investment percentages, and use fund hyperlinks or ticker symbols to research the available investments.

faq_pairs.question: How can a participant view statements?
faq_pairs.answer: The participant should select the three dots in the top right corner of the dashboard, then select Statements.

faq_pairs.question: When are statements generally posted?
faq_pairs.answer: Statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter.

faq_pairs.question: Can a participant manage future statement delivery?
faq_pairs.answer: Yes. The article says the participant can manage future statement delivery from the Statements section.

faq_pairs.question: How can a participant add or update beneficiaries?
faq_pairs.answer: The participant should select the three dots in the top right corner of the dashboard, then select Beneficiaries.

faq_pairs.question: When does the beneficiary feature become available?
faq_pairs.answer: The beneficiary feature becomes available once the participant's first contribution has been deposited.

faq_pairs.question: What allocation is required if a participant adds multiple beneficiaries?
faq_pairs.answer: If the participant adds multiple beneficiaries, the total allocation must equal 100%.

faq_pairs.question: What is a primary beneficiary?
faq_pairs.answer: A primary beneficiary is the person or people who receive benefits from the plan in the event of the participant's passing.

faq_pairs.question: What is a contingent beneficiary?
faq_pairs.answer: A contingent beneficiary is the person or people who may receive benefits if the primary beneficiary is deceased.

faq_pairs.question: Can a participant access loans and withdrawals from the dashboard?
faq_pairs.answer: If the plan allows loans or withdrawals and the participant is eligible, they can select Loans & Distributions from the top right corner of the dashboard.

faq_pairs.question: What withdrawal options can a participant select?
faq_pairs.answer: For withdrawals, the participant may select from the eligible withdrawal or termination distribution reasons shown, if the plan allows withdrawals and the participant is eligible.

faq_pairs.question: What loan information is shown in Loans & Distributions?
faq_pairs.answer: For loans, the maximum amount available to borrow will be displayed if loans are allowed by the plan and the participant is eligible.

faq_pairs.question: Can a participant use a loan calculator?
faq_pairs.answer: Yes. The article says the participant may access the Quick Loan Calculator and review loan specifications under What you should know before proceeding.

faq_pairs.question: When should a participant contact ForUsAll Participant Support?
faq_pairs.answer: The participant should contact ForUsAll Participant Support if they have trouble creating their account, do not receive their activation email, are unsure which email address to use, or need help with MFA setup.

faq_pairs.question: What is the ForUsAll Participant Support email?
faq_pairs.answer: The support email listed in the article is help@forusall.com.

faq_pairs.question: What is the ForUsAll Participant Support phone number?
faq_pairs.answer: The support phone number listed in the article is 844-401-2253.

definitions.definition: A required security step during account setup that requires a one-time authentication code in addition to the participant's email address and password.
A one-time six-digit code generated by an authenticator app on the participant's phone during MFA setup or future login.
A mobile app used to generate the one-time authentication code required for MFA. The article lists Google Authenticator and Okta Verify as supported authenticator apps.

A registration link sent by email when the email address and any required identity verification information match the system. The participant follows this link or registration flow to complete registration and create a password. In this article, the terms 'activation link' and 'activation email' refer to the same registration message: the 'activation email' is the email that delivers the 'activation link'.
The registration email that delivers the activation link. Used interchangeably with 'activation link' in this article.
A notice that helps confirm the participant can set up their ForUsAll online account, even if they are not yet eligible to contribute.

An internal communication indicator visible in the Communications section of the participant profile that confirms the participant has been sent an onboarding email. Used alongside profile availability to confirm whether account setup can proceed; the absence of an onboarding_email alone is not a definitive blocker if other setup indicators show the participant can proceed.

The participant's online account dashboard, which may include account balance, savings rate, portfolio snapshot, transaction history, links to change rates, My Portfolio, Loans & Distributions, Statements, Beneficiary Information, FAQ, and Settings.
The participant's contribution election, which may include pre-tax contributions, Roth contributions, or both.
A dashboard option the participant can choose when updating savings rates, then save by selecting Update.
The dashboard section where the participant can review available fund options, update investment percentages, and use fund hyperlinks or ticker symbols to research available investments.
The dashboard section where participants can view recent statements and manage future statement delivery.
The person or people who receive benefits from the plan in the event of the participant's passing.
The person or people who may receive benefits if the primary beneficiary is deceased.
The dashboard section participants may use to access loans or withdrawals if the plan allows them and the participant is eligible.
A tool available in the loan flow that participants may access when loans are allowed by the plan and the participant is eligible.

The ForUsAll support team reached at help@forusall.com or 844-401-2253 Monday-Friday, 7:00 AM-5:00 PM PST. Used in this article interchangeably with 'ForUsAll Participant Support'. Handles account-setup, email-mismatch, activation-email, and MFA-setup escalations; MFA reset requests specifically must be handled by phone and cannot be completed by email or chat (see the MFA article).

must_not: Do not treat the absence of an initial enrollment notice alone as a definitive blocker if internal account setup indicators show the participant can proceed.
Do not say an email address will work unless it matches the email the employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll.
Do not provide any activation email timing that is not stated in the article.
Do not suggest an MFA app other than Google Authenticator or Okta Verify based on this article.
Do not tell the participant to use Okta Mobile for MFA setup.
Do not guarantee that every dashboard feature listed in the article will appear for every participant.
Do not guarantee that the plan allows loans or withdrawals.
Do not conclude that a participant is eligible for a loan or withdrawal unless plan allowance and participant eligibility are confirmed outside this article.
Do not say the beneficiary feature is available before the participant's first contribution has been deposited.
Do not provide statement posting timing other than the article's statement that statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter.
Do not say a participant must be eligible to contribute before creating the online account; contribution eligibility is not required for account setup.
Do not guarantee that the participant will receive an activation link unless the email address matches the system.
Do not provide unsupported troubleshooting steps for a missing activation email; direct the participant to ForUsAll Participant Support if they do not receive it.
Do not describe MFA as optional; the article says ForUsAll requires MFA during account setup.
Do not omit that future logins require the authentication code from the app used during setup.

Do not instruct the participant to use a phone only for the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow; the article recommends using both a computer or laptop and a phone, and internal process treats a second device as needed for supported setup.
Do not guarantee that the participant can enter a savings rate as a dollar value; the article says that option is available only if the option is available.
Do not guarantee that the participant can access Beneficiaries before the first contribution has been deposited.
Do not guarantee that multiple beneficiary allocations can be saved unless the total allocation equals 100%.

Do not provide loan limits, withdrawal eligibility rules, distribution reasons, taxes, penalties, or processing timelines unless they are shown to the participant in Loans & Distributions or explicitly stated in the article.

Do not claim that ForUsAll Participant Support can change the participant's employer or payroll provider email on file; the article only says participants should contact HR or Participant Support if unsure which email to use.
Do not add or change support hours unless they are included through approved internal disclosure standards outside the article.
must_do_if_unsure: If the participant is unsure whether they can create an account yet, check whether their profile is available for setup and whether an onboarding_email is visible; redirect to support if setup status is unclear.
If the participant is having trouble creating their password, direct them to follow the prompts in the activation email and contact ForUsAll Participant Support if they cannot complete setup.
If the participant cannot scan the MFA QR code, tell them they may use the option to enter a setup key manually.
If the participant is using Okta Mobile, redirect them to use Okta Verify instead.
If the participant asks how to log in after setup, tell them to use their email address, password, and the authentication code from the app used during MFA setup.

If the participant's MFA status already shows enrolled, do not guide them through creating a new account; first guide them to log in with the MFA code from the app used during their previous setup. If they cannot access that code (for example, new phone, lost phone, deleted app, or code no longer appears), route them to the MFA reset process documented in 'LT: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for Your ForUsAll 401(k) Account', which requires a phone call to the ForUsAll Participant Support Team at 844-401-2253.
If the participant asks why a dashboard feature is missing, explain that the dashboard may include different features and do not guarantee that every listed feature is available.
If the participant asks why Beneficiaries is not available, confirm whether their first contribution has been deposited before stating the feature should be available.
If the participant asks about multiple beneficiaries, remind them that the total allocation must equal 100%.
If the participant asks why a statement is not available yet, use only the article's timeline that statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter.
If the participant asks about savings rates, explain the supported dashboard path and note that the dollar value option should only be mentioned as available if that option appears.
If the participant asks about portfolio changes, direct them to My Portfolio and Explore Funds as stated in the article.

internal_articles: LT: Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA) for Your ForUsAll 401(k) Account
LT: How to Access and Manage Your ForUsAll 401(k) Statements and Beneficiaries
LT: 401(k) Loan Complete Guide — Submission, Repayment & Support
LT: How to Request a 401(k) Termination Cash Withdrawal or Rollover

must_have.why_needed: Required to confirm that we found a user related to the participant in our system.
Required to confirm if there is a Primary Email on file. This email must be the same one their employer has in their payroll system or data. If the Primary Email field is empty, redirect to support.

must_have.meaning: The Record Keeper of the Participant.
must_have.why_needed: This article and instructions are intended for LT Trust plans. If RK is not LT Trust, and the participant is inquiring about creating an online account, redirect to support.

must_have.meaning: The status of the participant in the system.
must_have.why_needed: Required to confirm the participant status for context during online account setup. This does not determine whether they can create an account.

must_have.meaning: The status of the participant's MFA enrollment.
must_have.why_needed: To verify whether the participant may be creating an online account for the first time. If MFA is not enrolled, the participant can proceed with online account setup. If MFA status is enrolled, treat this as an indication that the participant may already have an account and should be guided toward logging in or redirected to support if they cannot use the existing MFA code.

must_have.meaning: Whether the participant has a mobile phone available for account setup.
example_values: Yes, I have a phone with me
No, I do not have a phone with me
must_have.why_needed: The article lists a mobile phone as needed and says the authenticator app generates the MFA code on the phone. If they confirm they do not have a phone, are not willing to do it or are having issues, redirect to support.
must_have.source_note: Supported by article section: Before You Start and Step 4: Set Up Multi-Factor Authentication

must_have.meaning: Whether the participant has a second device available for the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow, such as a computer, laptop, tablet/iPad, or another phone separate from the phone with the authenticator app.
must_have.why_needed: The website displays an MFA QR code that is scanned with the authenticator app on the participant's phone. For the standard supported first-time setup flow, participants need a second device to display the QR code. If they do not have a second device, redirect to support.
must_have.source_note: Supported by article section: Prerequisites and Step 4: Set Up Multi-Factor Authentication; internal process treats this as required for the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow.

nice_to_have.meaning: Whether the participant has received the initial enrollment notice or has an onboarding_email visible in the participant profile that helps confirm account setup timing.
nice_to_have.why_needed: The article says the account can be set up once the participant receives the initial enrollment notice, and internal review may also use onboarding_email or profile setup indicators to confirm whether setup can proceed.
nice_to_have.source_note: Supported by article section: When Can I Create My Account?

nice_to_have.data_point: Access to the account email address
nice_to_have.meaning: Whether the participant can access the email inbox connected to their ForUsAll 401(k) account.
example_values: Yes, I can access that email inbox
No, I cannot access that email inbox
nice_to_have.why_needed: The participant must open the activation email and follow the prompts to create a password. Preferably, they should access the activation email from the same computer or device used to request the activation link.
nice_to_have.source_note: Supported by article section: Before You Start and Step 3: Create Your Password

nice_to_have.meaning: Whether the participant has or can use an authenticator app listed in the article.
nice_to_have.why_needed: ForUsAll requires MFA during account setup, and the authentication code is generated by an authenticator app.
nice_to_have.source_note: Supported by article section: Step 4: Set Up Multi-Factor Authentication

nice_to_have.meaning: Which supported authenticator app the participant wants to use for setup.
nice_to_have.why_needed: The setup steps differ depending on whether the participant uses Google Authenticator or Okta Verify.
nice_to_have.source_note: Supported by article section: Option A and Option B

nice_to_have.meaning: Whether the participant's first contribution has been deposited. Must check on the recent Payrolls for the participant profile.
nice_to_have.why_needed: The beneficiary feature becomes available once the first contribution has been deposited.
nice_to_have.source_note: Supported by article section: Add or Update Beneficiaries. Must be the very first registered contribution.

nice_to_have.meaning: Whether the participant's plan allows the requested loan or withdrawal feature. For loans, this may include loan-specific plan details such as the maximum number of active loans allowed.
nice_to_have.why_needed: Loans & Distributions is available only if the plan allows the requested loan or withdrawal feature and the participant is eligible. If unsure, redirect to support to confirm.
nice_to_have.source_note: Supported by article section: Access Loans and Withdrawals. Plan allowance must be confirmed before directing the participant to use loan or withdrawal features.

nice_to_have.data_point: Participant eligibility for loans or withdrawals
nice_to_have.meaning: Whether the participant is eligible for the loan or withdrawal feature they are asking about.
example_values: A value visible in participant or account systems
nice_to_have.why_needed: Loans & Distributions is available only if the plan allows loans or withdrawals and the participant is eligible.
nice_to_have.source_note: Supported by article section: Access Loans and Withdrawals. Refer the participant to the article '401(k) Loan Basics and Support Guide'. Must redirect to support if unsure.

if_missing.agent_note: Retrieve from admin/portal systems (ForUsBots); do not ask participant. Confirm the participant profile using first name, last name, company name, and email before proceeding.
Retrieve from admin/portal systems (ForUsBots); do not ask participant. Confirm whether there is a Primary Email on file. If the Primary Email field is empty, redirect to support.

Retrieve from admin/portal systems (ForUsBots); do not ask participant. This article applies to LT Trust plans. If the record keeper is not LT Trust and the participant is asking about creating an online account, redirect to support.
Retrieve from admin/portal systems (ForUsBots); do not ask participant. Participant status should be checked for context, but it does not determine whether they can create an online account.

Retrieve from admin/portal systems (ForUsBots); do not ask participant. If MFA is not enrolled, the participant can proceed with online account setup. If MFA status is enrolled, treat this as an indication that the participant may already have an account and should be guided toward logging in or redirected to support if they cannot use the existing MFA code.

if_missing.ask_participant: Do you have a mobile phone available to complete MFA setup?
if_missing.agent_note: The authenticator app on the participant's phone generates the MFA code. If the participant confirms they do not have a phone, are not willing to complete MFA setup, or are having issues completing setup, redirect to support.

if_missing.ask_participant: Do you have a second device available, such as a computer, laptop, tablet, iPad, or another phone, so the MFA QR code can be displayed and scanned with your mobile phone?
if_missing.agent_note: For the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow, the participant needs a second device to display the MFA QR code while scanning it with the authenticator app on their phone. If the participant does not have a second device, redirect them to ForUsAll Participant Support.

if_missing.missing_data_point: Access to the account email address
if_missing.ask_participant: Do you have access to the inbox for the email address connected to your ForUsAll account?
if_missing.agent_note: The participant must be able to open the activation email to create their password. If they cannot access the inbox, do not proceed with setup; direct them to ForUsAll Participant Support.

if_missing.ask_participant: Do you have Google Authenticator or Okta Verify available, or can you install one of them on your phone for MFA setup?
if_missing.agent_note: ForUsAll requires MFA during setup, and the authentication code is generated by an authenticator app. If the participant cannot or will not install Google Authenticator or Okta Verify, redirect them to ForUsAll Participant Support; do not suggest other authenticator apps based on this article.

if_missing.ask_participant: Would you like to use Google Authenticator or Okta Verify for MFA setup?
if_missing.agent_note: The setup steps differ between the two apps. Use the participant's selection to choose the Google Authenticator or Okta Verify step. If the participant has no preference, default to whichever they already have installed; if neither is installed, briefly describe both before they choose.

Retrieve from admin/portal systems (ForUsBots) by reviewing the most recent payrolls on the participant profile; do not ask the participant. Until the first contribution is confirmed as deposited, do not tell the participant the Beneficiaries feature should be available. If the first contribution status cannot be confirmed, explain that Beneficiaries becomes available once the first contribution is deposited and offer to re-check after the next pay date.

Retrieve from the plan profile; do not ask the participant. If the plan profile does not clearly show loan or withdrawal allowance, do not direct the participant to Loans & Distributions and do not guarantee availability; redirect to ForUsAll Participant Support to confirm plan rules.

if_missing.missing_data_point: Participant eligibility for loans or withdrawals
if_missing.agent_note: Retrieve from the participant profile and account systems; do not ask the participant for eligibility rules. If eligibility cannot be confirmed, do not conclude the participant can use Loans & Distributions; redirect to ForUsAll Participant Support and refer the participant to '401(k) Loan Basics and Support Guide' for general loan context.

disambiguation_notes: The article uses multi-factor authentication and MFA to refer to the same required security step during account setup.
MFA requires a one-time authentication code in addition to the participant's email address and password.
The authentication code is generated by an authenticator app on the participant's phone.
Google Authenticator and Okta Verify are the only authenticator apps listed in the article.
Okta Verify is the supported Okta app in this article; Okta Mobile is specifically not supported for this setup.
For Google Authenticator, the article identifies the correct app by the multicolor star icon.
For Okta Verify, the article identifies the correct app by a blue circle with a check mark.
If the participant cannot scan the MFA QR code, the article allows using the manual setup key option.

The article recommends using both a computer or laptop and a phone for MFA setup because the QR code displays on the computer and is scanned with the authenticator app on the phone; internal process treats a second device as needed for the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow.
The participant's email must match the email their employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll.
If the participant is unsure which email address is associated with their account, the article directs them to contact their HR team or ForUsAll Participant Support.
If the email address does not match the system, the article directs the participant to contact their HR manager or ForUsAll Participant Support.

Account setup can happen once the participant profile is available for account setup; receiving an initial enrollment notice or seeing an onboarding_email is helpful confirmation, and contribution eligibility is not required.
The activation link is sent only if the email address matches the system.
The dashboard may include listed features, so the agent must not guarantee that every listed feature appears for every participant.
Savings rates may be entered as a percentage, or switched to a dollar value only if that option is available.
The beneficiary feature becomes available once the participant's first contribution has been deposited.
If multiple beneficiaries are added, the total allocation must equal 100%.

Primary beneficiary and contingent beneficiary are different beneficiary types; the primary beneficiary receives benefits if the participant passes away, while the contingent beneficiary may receive benefits if the primary beneficiary is deceased.
Loans and withdrawals are plan- and eligibility-dependent, so the agent must not treat them as universally available.
For withdrawals, the participant may select only from the eligible withdrawal or termination distribution reasons shown in Loans & Distributions.
For loans, the maximum amount available to borrow is displayed in the loan flow when loans are available to the participant.
Statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter; the agent must not provide a different statement availability timeline.

eligibility_requirements: The participant profile is available for account setup; receiving the initial enrollment notice or seeing an onboarding_email is helpful confirmation but is not the only way to confirm setup can proceed.
The participant has a ForUsAll profile associated with their first name, last name, company name, and email address.
The participant's email address matches the email their employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll.
The participant has access to the email inbox associated with their ForUsAll 401(k) account so they can open the activation email and complete registration.
The participant has a mobile phone available for MFA setup because the authentication code is generated by an authenticator app on the phone.

The participant has a second device available for the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow, such as a computer, laptop, tablet, iPad, or another phone, so the QR code can be displayed and scanned with the authenticator app.
The participant completes required MFA setup using Google Authenticator or Okta Verify.
If the participant uses Google Authenticator, they use the app identified by the multicolor star icon.
If the participant uses Okta, they use Okta Verify, identified by a blue circle with a check mark, and not Okta Mobile.
For future logins, the participant must use their email address, password, and the authentication code from the app used during MFA setup.
Beneficiary updates are available once the participant's first contribution has been deposited.
If the participant adds multiple beneficiaries, the total beneficiary allocation must equal 100%.
Loans or withdrawals are available only if the plan allows them and the participant is eligible.
For withdrawals, the participant may select from the eligible withdrawal or termination distribution reasons shown in Loans & Distributions.
For loans, the maximum amount available to borrow is displayed in the loan flow when loans are available to the participant.
blocking_conditions: No ForUsAll participant profile can be found for the participant using the available identifying information.
The participant's record keeper is not LT Trust and the participant is asking for LT Trust-specific online account setup guidance.
The participant does not have a Primary Email on file.
The email address entered does not match the system or does not match the email the employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll.
The participant does not have access to the connected email address needed to open the activation email.
The participant cannot open the activation email or complete the password creation prompts.
The participant does not have a mobile phone available for MFA setup.
The participant does not have access to a second device needed for the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow.
The participant cannot or does not want to complete required MFA setup.
The participant is trying to use Okta Mobile instead of Okta Verify for MFA setup.
The participant's MFA status already shows enrolled, indicating they may already have an account, and they are trying to create a new online account instead of logging in with the existing MFA setup.
The plan does not allow the requested loan or withdrawal, or the participant is not eligible for the requested loan or withdrawal.
The participant's first contribution has not been deposited and the participant is trying to use the beneficiary feature.
The participant is trying to add multiple beneficiaries but the total beneficiary allocation does not equal 100%.

missing_data_conditions.condition: The participant's name is missing from the internal participant profile lookup.
The participant's email is missing from the internal participant profile lookup or the Primary Email field is empty.
The participant's record keeper is missing from the internal participant profile lookup.
The participant status is missing from the internal participant profile lookup.
The participant's MFA status is missing from the internal participant profile lookup.
The participant has not confirmed whether they have a mobile phone available for MFA setup.
missing_data_conditions.ask_participant: Do you have a mobile phone available to complete MFA setup?

missing_data_conditions.condition: The participant has not confirmed whether they have a second device available for the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow.
missing_data_conditions.ask_participant: Do you have a second device available, such as a computer, laptop, tablet, iPad, or another phone, so the MFA QR code can be displayed and scanned with your mobile phone?

missing_data_conditions.condition: The participant has not confirmed whether they have access to the email inbox connected to their ForUsAll account.
missing_data_conditions.missing_data_point: Access to the account email address
missing_data_conditions.ask_participant: Do you have access to the inbox for the email address connected to your ForUsAll account?

missing_data_conditions.condition: The participant has not confirmed whether they can use one of the supported authenticator apps.
missing_data_conditions.ask_participant: Do you have Google Authenticator or Okta Verify available for MFA setup?

missing_data_conditions.condition: The participant has not selected which supported MFA app they want to use.
missing_data_conditions.ask_participant: Would you like to use Google Authenticator or Okta Verify for MFA setup?

missing_data_conditions.condition: The participant is asking about Beneficiaries, but it is unknown whether their first contribution has been deposited.
The participant is asking about loans or withdrawals, but it is unknown whether the plan allows loans or withdrawals.
The participant is asking about loans or withdrawals, but their eligibility for the requested loan or withdrawal feature is unknown.
missing_data_conditions.missing_data_point: Participant eligibility for loans or withdrawals

allowed_conclusions: The participant can set up the online account once their profile is available for account setup; receiving the initial enrollment notice or seeing an onboarding_email is helpful confirmation, and contribution eligibility is not required for account setup.
The participant needs access to the email address connected to the ForUsAll 401(k) account.
The participant's email address must match the email their employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll.

If the email address and any required identity verification information match the system, the participant will receive or continue through an activation flow with instructions to complete registration.
If the email does not match the system, or if the participant is unsure which email address to use, they should contact their HR manager or ForUsAll Participant Support.
After completing identity verification if prompted and creating a password, the participant should return to forusall.com/login and log in using their email address and new password.
ForUsAll requires MFA during account setup.
MFA requires a one-time authentication code in addition to the participant's email address and password.
The authentication code is generated by an authenticator app on the participant's phone.
Google Authenticator and Okta Verify are supported authenticator apps listed in the article.
For Google Authenticator, the correct app icon is the multicolor star.
For Okta Verify, the correct app icon is a blue circle with a check mark.
The participant should not use Okta Mobile for this MFA setup.
The participant can use the manual setup key option if they cannot scan the QR code.

For the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow, the participant should complete MFA setup using both a computer or laptop and their phone; if they do not have a second device, direct them to ForUsAll Participant Support.
For future logins, the participant must enter their email address, password, and the authentication code from the app used during setup.
Once logged in, the participant will see their ForUsAll dashboard.

The dashboard may include account balance, savings rate, portfolio snapshot, transaction history, links to change rates, My Portfolio, Loans & Distributions, Statements, Beneficiary Information, FAQ, and Settings.
The participant can update their pre-tax contribution, Roth contribution, or both from Change My Savings Rates.
The participant may enter their savings rate as a percentage, or switch to a dollar value if that option is available.
The participant can choose to enroll in auto-escalation while updating savings rates.
The participant can review available fund options, update investment percentages, and use fund hyperlinks or ticker symbols from My Portfolio and Explore Funds.
The participant can view recent statements from the Statements section.
Statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter.
The participant can manage future statement delivery from the Statements section.
The beneficiary feature becomes available once the first contribution has been deposited.
ForUsAll recommends adding at least one beneficiary as soon as possible once the feature is available.
If the participant adds multiple beneficiaries, the total allocation must equal 100%.
A primary beneficiary is the person or people who receive benefits from the plan in the event of the participant's passing.
A contingent beneficiary is the person or people who may receive benefits if the primary beneficiary is deceased.
Loans & Distributions may be used if the plan allows loans or withdrawals and the participant is eligible.
For withdrawals, the participant may select from the eligible withdrawal or termination distribution reasons shown.
For loans, the maximum amount available to borrow will be displayed if loans are available to the participant.
The participant may access the Quick Loan Calculator and review loan specifications under What you should know before proceeding.

The participant should contact ForUsAll Participant Support if they have trouble creating their account, do not receive their activation email, are unsure which email address to use, or need help with MFA setup.
not_allowed_conclusions: Do not guarantee the plan allows loans or withdrawals.
Do not guarantee the participant is eligible for a loan or withdrawal.
Do not guarantee that every listed dashboard feature will appear for every participant.
Do not say the participant can add or update beneficiaries before the first contribution has been deposited.
Do not say multiple beneficiary allocations can be saved unless the total allocation equals 100%.
Do not say Okta Mobile can be used for this MFA setup.
Do not suggest MFA apps other than Google Authenticator or Okta Verify based on this article.
Do not describe MFA as optional; the article states that ForUsAll requires MFA during account setup.
Do not say the participant can complete future logins without the authentication code from the app used during MFA setup.
Do not guarantee that the participant will receive an activation link unless the email address matches the system.
Do not say an email address will work unless it matches the email the employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll.
Do not provide an activation email delivery timeframe that is not stated in the article.
Do not treat the absence of an initial enrollment notice alone as a definitive blocker if internal account setup indicators show the participant can proceed.
Do not say the participant must be eligible to contribute before creating the online account.
Do not guarantee that the participant can enter a savings rate as a dollar value; the article says this is available only if that option is available.
Do not provide statement posting timing other than the article's statement that statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter.

Do not provide loan limits, withdrawal eligibility rules, distribution reasons, tax guidance, penalty guidance, or processing timelines unless they are shown to the participant in Loans & Distributions or explicitly stated in the article.
Do not provide any fee information because the article does not mention fees.
Do not add or change support hours unless they are included through approved internal disclosure standards outside the article.

Do not claim ForUsAll Participant Support can update the employer or payroll provider email on file; the article only says to contact HR or Participant Support if the participant is unsure which email to use.
Do not include ticket actions, tagging, escalation, routing, or internal ticket workflow instructions in participant-facing conclusions.

participant_message_components: You can set up your ForUsAll online account at forusall.com/login once your profile is available for account setup, even if you are not yet eligible to contribute.
Use the email address connected to your ForUsAll 401(k) account. It must match the email your employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll.
If your email address and any required identity verification information match the system, you will receive or continue through an activation flow with instructions to complete registration.
Follow the prompts, complete identity verification if prompted, and create your password.
ForUsAll requires MFA during setup. MFA uses a one-time authentication code from an authenticator app on your phone in addition to your email address and password.
You can use Google Authenticator or Okta Verify for MFA setup.
For future logins, you will use your email address, password, and the authentication code from the app used during setup.
next_steps: Go to forusall.com/login.
Select New here? Create an Account at the bottom of the login page.
Enter the email address associated with your ForUsAll account.
Follow the registration prompts and verify your identity using your last name and the last 4 digits of your Social Security number if prompted.
Open the activation email if sent, follow the prompts to create your password, and then return to forusall.com/login using your email address and new password.
After logging in, when the website displays the MFA QR code, open Google Authenticator or Okta Verify on your phone.
Scan the QR code with the authenticator app and enter the six-digit code on the ForUsAll website.
If you cannot scan the QR code, use the option to enter a setup key manually.

After setup is complete, use the dashboard to access available features such as savings rate changes, My Portfolio, statements, beneficiaries, loans, and distributions when those features are available and applicable.
warnings: For the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow, use both a computer or laptop and your phone because the QR code displays on one device and is scanned with the authenticator app on your phone.
Use Google Authenticator with the multicolor star icon if choosing Google Authenticator.
Use Okta Verify with the blue circle and check mark icon if choosing Okta.
Do not use Okta Mobile for this MFA setup.
Dashboard features may vary, so not every listed feature is guaranteed to appear for every participant.
Loans and withdrawals are available only if the plan allows them and the participant is eligible.
The beneficiary feature becomes available once the first contribution has been deposited.
Statements are generally posted by the end of the month following each calendar quarter.
what_not_to_say: Do not promise that every dashboard feature will be available.
Do not guarantee that the plan allows loans or withdrawals.
Do not guarantee that the participant is eligible for a loan or withdrawal.
Do not say the participant can add or update beneficiaries before the first contribution has been deposited.
Do not say Okta Mobile can be used for MFA setup.
Do not suggest authenticator apps other than Google Authenticator or Okta Verify based on this article.
Do not describe MFA as optional.
Do not give a timeline for receiving the activation email because the article does not state one.
Do not provide fee information because the article does not mention fees.
Do not provide loan limits, withdrawal eligibility rules, tax guidance, penalty guidance, or processing timelines unless they are explicitly shown to the participant or stated in the article.

participant_message_components: I need one or more details before confirming the right setup steps.
Your email must match the email your employer or payroll provider has on file with ForUsAll.
ForUsAll requires MFA during setup, so you will need a supported authenticator app on your phone.

For the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow, you will need a second device, such as a computer, laptop, tablet, iPad, or another phone, so the MFA QR code can be displayed and scanned with the authenticator app on your phone.
next_steps: Confirm whether the participant profile is available for account setup, including whether an onboarding_email is visible in the participant profile.
Confirm the participant profile internally using first name, last name, company name, and email address.
Confirm that the participant's Primary Email is present in the profile.
Confirm that the email being used for registration matches the Primary Email on file.
Confirm that the participant can access the inbox for the email address connected to their ForUsAll account.
Confirm the participant's record keeper is LT Trust before using this article's LT Trust-specific setup guidance.
Confirm the participant's MFA status before deciding whether they are creating an account for the first time or should log in with an existing MFA setup.
Confirm that the participant has a mobile phone available for MFA setup.
Confirm that the participant has a second device available for the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow.
Confirm that the participant can use Google Authenticator or Okta Verify.
warnings: If the email address does not match, or if the participant is unsure which email to use, direct them to their HR manager or ForUsAll Participant Support.
If the participant's Primary Email field is empty, redirect to ForUsAll Participant Support.
Do not disclose the email address on file to the participant if the email they provide does not match; simply explain that it does not match and direct them to support.

If the participant's MFA status already shows enrolled, do not immediately guide them through creating a new account; explain that they may already have an account and should try logging in with the MFA code they previously set up.
If the participant is having issues with an existing MFA code, redirect them to ForUsAll Participant Support.
If the participant cannot use a mobile phone or second device for the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow, redirect them to ForUsAll Participant Support.
questions_to_ask: Have you received your initial enrollment notice, or are you asking because you have not received it yet?
What email address are you using to create your ForUsAll account?
Do you have access to the inbox for the email address connected to your ForUsAll account?
Do you have a mobile phone available to complete MFA setup?
Do you have a second device available, such as a computer, laptop, tablet, iPad, or another phone, so the MFA QR code can be displayed and scanned with your mobile phone?
Do you have Google Authenticator or Okta Verify available for MFA setup?
Would you like to use Google Authenticator or Okta Verify for MFA setup?
what_not_to_say: Do not treat the missing initial enrollment notice alone as a definitive blocker if internal setup indicators show the participant can proceed.
Do not say the email will work unless it matches the employer or payroll provider email on file with ForUsAll.
Do not say or disclose what email is on file if the participant provides an email that does not match; simply tell them it does not match and redirect to support.
Do not ask the participant to provide internal profile data that should be retrieved from admin or portal systems.

Do not immediately guide a participant through new account creation if MFA status already shows enrolled; first guide them toward logging in or redirect them to support if they cannot use the existing MFA code.
Do not tell the participant they can complete the standard supported first-time MFA setup flow from one cellphone only.
Do not suggest MFA apps other than Google Authenticator or Okta Verify based on this article.
Do not tell the participant to use Okta Mobile for MFA setup.

participant_message_components: Account setup can happen once your profile is available for account setup, even if you are not yet eligible to contribute.
If you have not received your initial enrollment notice, the agent should confirm internal setup indicators before deciding whether setup can proceed.
Loans and withdrawals are available only if your plan allows them and you are eligible.
The beneficiary feature becomes available once your first contribution has been deposited.
If your email does not match the system, you may need to contact your HR manager or ForUsAll Participant Support before continuing.
next_steps: For account setup, confirm whether the participant profile is available for setup and whether an onboarding_email is visible in the participant profile.
For email mismatch issues, do not disclose the email on file; direct the participant to their HR manager or ForUsAll Participant Support.
If the participant does not have a Primary Email on file, redirect to ForUsAll Participant Support.
If the participant's record keeper is not LT Trust and they are asking about this LT Trust-specific account setup process, redirect to support.
If the participant's MFA status already shows enrolled, explain that they may already have an account and should try logging in using the MFA code they previously set up.
If the participant has issues with an existing MFA code, redirect to ForUsAll Participant Support.
For beneficiary access, confirm whether the participant's first contribution has been deposited.
